text: Add src/client/ApiEaseDeleteRequestClient.js as the canonical Node client for deleting a single request by id.
goal: Implement apiease read, apiease update, and apiease delete as public CLI commands.
context: The public CRUD exercise already uses DELETE api/v1/resources/requests/:requestId for request cleanup.
Delete needs the same base URL normalization and authentication headers as the other request clients.
steps: Implement a deleteRequest method that accepts apiBaseUrl, apiKey, shopDomain, and requestId.
Call the versioned request item endpoint with DELETE and no request body.
Return structured success and failure results that the CLI can render in human-readable and JSON modes.
constraints: Keep the delete flow focused on request resources only.
Do not require a file input or request payload for delete.
acceptanceCriteria: The client issues DELETE requests to api/v1/resources/requests/<encoded requestId> using the standard APIEase authentication headers.
Successful backend responses are surfaced as structured success results without inventing extra CLI-only fields.
Backend failures, transport errors, and non-JSON failures return ok false results with machine-readable status, errorCode, message, and fieldErrors.
